text = contents['/mnt/data/file-LFb3VhjU4t5ReVZypipXWC-Q032.txt']
len_text = len(text)
quote_positions = [i for i, ch in enumerate(text) if ch == '"']
len_text, len(quote_positions), quote_positions[:10]

quotes = []
for i in range(0, len(quote_positions), 2):
    start = quote_positions[i]+1
    end = quote_positions[i+1]
    quotes.append((start, end, text[start:end]))
quotes

def split_ayahs(quote_tuple):
    start, end, s = quote_tuple
    parts = [p.strip() for p in s.split('*')]
    # Now compute absolute spans for each part within the original quote.
    spans = []
    idx = 0
    for part in parts:
        # find part starting at idx in s
        rel = s.find(part, idx)
        if rel == -1:
            rel = s.find(part)  # fallback
        abs_start = start + rel
        abs_end = abs_start + len(part)
        spans.append((abs_start, abs_end, part))
        idx = rel + len(part)
    return spans

split1 = split_ayahs(quotes[0])
split2 = split_ayahs(quotes[2])
split1, split2

# Let's assemble the citations list as per the schema.
text = contents['/mnt/data/file-LFb3VhjU4t5ReVZypipXWC-Q032.txt']

# Pre-determined spans:
# Ayah splits
ayya_spans = [
    # Al-Mu'minun 23:12
    {"span": split1[0], "sura_name": "المؤمنون", "sura_number": 23, "aya_number": 12},
    # 23:13
    {"span": split1[1], "sura_name": "المؤمنون", "sura_number": 23, "aya_number": 13},
    # 23:14
    {"span": split1[2], "sura_name": "المؤمنون", "sura_number": 23, "aya_number": 14},
    # At-Talaq 65:2
    {"span": split2[0], "sura_name": "الطلاق", "sura_number": 65, "aya_number": 2},
    # 65:3
    {"span": split2[1], "sura_name": "الطلاق", "sura_number": 65, "aya_number": 3},
]

hadith_spans = [
    # "إن الله جميل يحب الجمال"
    {"start": quotes[1][0], "end": quotes[1][1], "collection_name": "Sahih Muslim", "book_number": 1, "hadith_number": 91},
    # "إن الله لا ينظر إلى صوركم..."
    {"start": quotes[3][0], "end": quotes[3][1], "collection_name": "Sahih Muslim", "book_number": 45, "hadith_number": 41},
]

citations = []
# Aya citations
for a in ayya_spans:
    s_abs, e_abs, span_text = a["span"]
    citations.append({
        "span_start": s_abs,
        "span_end": e_abs,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": True,
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": a["sura_name"],
            "sura_number": a["sura_number"],
            "aya_number": a["aya_number"],
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": span_text,
        "completely_invalid": False,
    })

# Hadith citations
for h in hadith_spans:
    s_abs = h["start"]
    e_abs = h["end"]
    span_text = text[s_abs:e_abs]
    citations.append({
        "span_start": s_abs,
        "span_end": e_abs,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": True,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {
            "collection_name": h["collection_name"],
            "book_number": h["book_number"],
            "hadith_number": h["hadith_number"],
        },
        "corrected_text": span_text,
        "completely_invalid": False,
    })

len(citations), citations[:2]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
